name: QNI : Suspicious Website Access
notes:
This rule triggers when a website categorized as suspicious by X-Force has been accessed.  

The URL is considered suspicious if the X-Force categorization for it returns one of the following: "Alcohol", "Anonymisation Services", "Botnet Command and Control Server", "Computer Crime / Hacking", "Cryptocurrency Mining", "Erotic / Sex", "Gambling / Lottery", "Illegal Activities", "Illegal Drugs", "Malware", "Phishing URLs", "Political Extreme / Hate / Discrimination", "Pornography", "Sects", "Spam URLs", "Violence / Extreme", or "Warez / Software Piracy".
conditions_text: when the flow matches Web Categories is not N/A

when any of Web Categories match (Alcohol|Anonymisation Services|Botnet Command and Control Server|Computer Crime / Hacking|Cryptocurrency Mining|Erotic / Sex|Gambling / Lottery|Illegal Activities|Illegal Drugs|Malware|Phishing URLs|Political Extreme / Hate / Discrimination|Pornography|Sects|Spam URLs|Violence / Extreme|Warez / Software Piracy)
TD_list: APPLY QNI : Suspicious Website Access on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the flow matches Web Categories is not N/A

AND when any of Web Categories match (Alcohol|Anonymisation Services|Botnet Command and Control Server|Computer Crime / Hacking|Cryptocurrency Mining|Erotic / Sex|Gambling / Lottery|Illegal Activities|Illegal Drugs|Malware|Phishing URLs|Political Extreme / Hate / Discrimination|Pornography|Sects|Spam URLs|Violence / Extreme|Warez / Software Piracy)
RED_list: Event name: QNI : Suspicious Website Accessed
Event description: Detects access to a website categorized as suspicious by X-Force.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
